insight: Cryo Ray is considered by community members to be among the strongest units in the game, with some arguing it needs a nerf (such as reducing to 2 health). Getting a Conduit is almost never wrong when Cryo Ray is available in the set.
author: pikachumemes.
context: Discussion about chill mechanics and whether frostbites and Cryo Ray are overpowered.

insight: Frostbites are dangerous primarily for the first bite — new players who don't know how to gambit against them end up letting their opponent trade cheap red units for expensive blue units, which is extremely unfavorable. Proper gambiting against frostbites is an important skill.
author: awaclus
context: Discussion about whether frostbites and chill mechanics are overpowered, and how new players struggle against them.

insight: Apollo (the snipe unit requiring triple Blastforge) is generally considered too slow and too committal to be consistently good. Its main value is sniping something worth more than the cost of building a third Blastforge, but the two-turn setup where you are doing little productive ('jackShiT') makes it weak in fast sets like Flame Animus.
author: consensus
context: Extended debate between pikachumemes. and awaclus about when Apollo is worth buying.

insight: Apollo is worth buying when it can snipe something sufficiently valuable — even wasting the third Blastforge for the rest of the game is acceptable if the snipe target is high-value enough (e.g., a Cynestra or a Tesla Coil). However, it is almost certainly not worth it in fast Animus sets because you simply do not have two turns to spend doing little.
author: awaclus
context: awaclus arguing the nuanced conditions under which Apollo is worth buying despite its slow setup.

insight: Apollo is actually great value against Ampo (Amphitheater/fast pressure unit), but you simply do not have the time to set it up — the two-turn 'jackShiT' turns are fatal against Ampo's speed, so the timing mismatch makes Apollo unplayable there despite its theoretical efficiency.
author: awaclus
context: Discussion of why Ampo beats Apollo despite Apollo being theoretically efficient against it.

insight: Frontline units are more powerful than their cost would suggest but as a drawback cannot be defended (blocked) and cannot absorb if the opponent has enough attack to kill them. Defenders with Frontline are an exception — the opponent can choose not to kill non-defender Frontline units.
author: awaclus
context: awaclus explaining the design purpose and mechanical tradeoff of the Frontline keyword.

insight: Chill is designed to provide the threat of breaching the opponent at a cheaper cost compared to raw damage. One Frostbite equals 3 chill (per velizar_), making Frostbites extremely efficient at generating chill pressure relative to their cost.
author: awaclus
context: awaclus explaining chill's design purpose; velizar_ separately noted the conversion rate of frostbites to chill.

insight: Perforator (also called Piercer) is worth buying for soak even in many non-obvious situations: its 2 health and 1 threat for 3R is better value than an Engineer for soak purposes because of the threat it provides, and it can be even better if you would waste red resources otherwise.
author: awaclus
context: awaclus making the case for buying Perforator/Piercer primarily as a soak unit rather than for its attack.

insight: Antima Comet is considered a poorly designed unit because when one player has the better Antima timing, the opponent has essentially no counterplay — attacking into the Engineer wall loses, and defending allows the opponent to stack attackers behind the Engineer wall, making both responses losing lines.
author: masn6811
context: masn6811 contrasting Antima Comet's sharp, uninteractive timing wins against the proposed Thunderbolt unit which they argue has more counterplay.

insight: A general rule of thumb when playing green against red is that extra damage on both sides favors the green player, making units that increase overall damage output (like the custom Recoil Ray concept) strategically interesting as red counters in green builds.
author: masn6811
context: masn6811 explaining the strategic rationale behind a custom unit designed to counter red strategies.

insight: When playing a big burst-damage unit like a custom Warship (buildtime 9), the optimal strategy is to delay the purchase as late as possible to exhaust the opponent's resources maximally while still guaranteeing you can complete it. In mirror situations, the first Warship gets earlier attacks but the second gets an effective golden shield.
author: masn6811
context: masn6811 explaining the strategic depth of their custom Warship unit design, illustrating broader principles about late-game burst timing.

insight: 'Vanilla mata' refers to standard ranked queue games in Prismata using the default unit pool, as opposed to custom or event sets.
author: masn6811
context: masn6811 clarifying what 'vanilla mata' means in response to a question from randomflyingtaco.

insight: The community consensus among experienced players is that the game plays best with BS+10 or more advanced units (silentslayers), though masn6811 argues that with sufficiently complex unit designs BS+8 can remain non-trivial. BS+8 on vanilla advanced units is considered too imbalanced/homogenous by silentslayers.
author: silentslayers
context: Discussion about the optimal number of advanced units in a Prismata set for strategic depth.

insight: A community member (.sha6001) requested a bot stronger than Master Bot at depth 7, suggesting the current Master Bot ceiling is not sufficient for experienced players.
author: .sha6001
context: Discussion about sniping Xaetron, during which sha requested a harder bot than Master Bot depth 7.

insight: Damaging Fragile units on breach and then sniping them on a subsequent turn is a noted advanced trick — it allows you to destroy units that would otherwise require prohibitive investment to snipe directly.
author: spiritfryer
context: spiritfryer mentioning advanced snipe techniques during a discussion about sniping Xaetron when the opponent misplays.
